title: PayPal vs Crypto for Selling Online When PayPal Is Not Available in Your Country — An Honest Comparison
author: Unchained Commerce
excerpt: PayPal, Stripe, Gumroad, and Payhip do not work in your country. That is a platform problem, not a you problem. Here is what actually works.

content.text: Having a great digital product is one thing, but being unable to collect payment for it is a whole different story, especially when platforms like PayPal are not available in your country, leaving you wondering how to sell online without them. This is a frustrating reality many creators in Nigeria, Ghana, and other restricted countries face every day. They have the products, they have the audience, but they lack the means to get paid. The traditional payment platforms have left them out, and it seems like there's no way to sell digital products without jumping through hoops. But what if there was a way to bypass these restrictions and get paid without relying on platforms that don't cater to your needs?
What You Are Actually Comparing

When comparing PayPal to crypto for selling online, it's essential to understand what you're actually comparing. On one hand, you have traditional payment platforms that rely on banking infrastructure and have strict compliance rules, which often exclude creators from certain countries. On the other hand, you have crypto-native infrastructure that operates independently of these restrictions, allowing for a more global and inclusive approach to online sales. The key difference lies in the underlying mechanics of how these platforms work and who they cater to.
Where the Traditional Platforms Break Down

Traditional platforms like PayPal, Stripe, Gumroad, and Payhip break down when it comes to serving creators in restricted countries. The reason for this is largely due to compliance decisions and fraud risk tiers, which lead to strict geographic restrictions. These platforms often require creators to have a bank account in a supported country or go through lengthy and sometimes impossible verification processes. This means that many creators are left without a reliable way to collect payments, making it difficult to sustain their businesses.
What Crypto-Native Infrastructure Does Differently

Crypto-native infrastructure, on the other hand, does things differently. By leveraging blockchain technology and cryptocurrencies, these platforms can operate without the need for traditional banking infrastructure. This means that creators can sell their digital products without worrying about geographic restrictions or compliance issues. Platforms like Unchained Commerce offer a suite of tools, including the Core Shop Engine, Web3 Wallet Connect, and Admin Dashboard, that make it easy for creators to set up their own digital stores and start selling without needing a traditional payment processor.
The reality is that traditional payment platforms like PayPal and Stripe are not designed to serve creators in restricted countries, leaving them without a reliable way to collect payments.
The Real Cost Difference

One of the most significant advantages of crypto-native infrastructure is the cost difference. Traditional platforms often charge high fees for transactions, and in some cases, creators may even need to pay for subscriptions or services they don't need. In contrast, crypto-native platforms typically have lower fees and often don't require creators to pay for anything beyond what they need. With Unchained Commerce, for example, creators can get started with a one-time payment and have lifetime access to the tools they need, without any recurring fees or subscriptions.
Which One to Use and Why

So, which one should you use and why? For creators in restricted countries, crypto-native infrastructure is often the better choice. It offers a more inclusive and global approach to online sales, without the restrictions and compliance issues that come with traditional platforms. By using a platform like Unchained Commerce, creators can focus on what they do best – creating great digital products – without worrying about how they'll get paid. With the right tools and infrastructure in place, creators can finally start selling their products and reaching their full potential.
What Creators in Restricted Countries Are Asking

If you're a creator in Nigeria looking for a way to sell digital products without PayPal, you're not alone. Many creators in restricted countries face the same challenge, and it's not just about finding a PayPal alternative. It's about finding a reliable way to collect payments and sustain your business. The good news is that there are options available, such as crypto-native infrastructure, that can help you bypass traditional payment platforms and start selling your products without restrictions. With the right tools and infrastructure in place, you can finally start getting paid for your creations and growing your business.
Crypto-native infrastructure offers a more inclusive and global approach to online sales, allowing creators to sell their digital products without restrictions or compliance issues.
Related Topics Worth Reading

For creators in restricted countries, finding the right platform to sell their digital products is just the beginning. They also need to consider how they'll market and promote their products, as well as how they'll handle customer support and other business operations. By using a platform like Unchained Commerce, creators can focus on these other aspects of their business, knowing that they have a reliable way to collect payments and sell their products.
Your Launch Checklist — No Platform Permission Required

Before launching your digital store, make sure you have the right tools and infrastructure in place. This includes setting up a secure and reliable payment system, such as the one offered by Unchained Commerce. You'll also want to make sure you have a way to deliver your digital products automatically, which is where the Core Shop Engine comes in. Additionally, you'll want to consider using Web3 Wallet Connect to integrate with multiple blockchain wallets and Admin Dashboard to manage your transactions and orders. By having these tools in place, you can ensure a smooth and successful launch for your digital store.
With the right tools and infrastructure in place, creators can finally start selling their products and reaching their full potential, without being held back by traditional payment platforms.
